mechanism_narrative: Persistent positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumption demand, which raises expected corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS changes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, elevated labor market slack suppresses wage-driven consumption growth and keeps equity risk premia elevated, generating consistently negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months when UNRATE exceeds 8.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint effect of declining industrial production and falling retail sales creates a contractionary demand shock that is stronger than either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate interaction of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns are not more negative than either univariate signal alone.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in housing starts predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon during post-crisis recovery periods because construction multipliers amplify demand expectations when credit markets are healing from a balance-sheet recession.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases fail to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances during high-unemployment recovery phases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as high-frequency activity surprises get priced in rapidly.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO accelerations are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, it signals demand overheating that pressures the central bank to tighten financial conditions, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as the discount rate on future cash flows rises.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between falling unemployment and rising retail sales confirms a consumption-driven expansion with labor market validation; this joint signal produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns stronger than either variable alone.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-RSAFS interaction term fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the bivariate signal underperforms the univariate RSAFS signal.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines month-over-month, the drop signals contracting manufacturing activity and inventory destocking, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as high-frequency data triggers rapid earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO month-over-month change is negative over any 2-year rolling window.
